title: Longest Subarray With Absolute Limit
description: You are given an array of integers nums and an integer limit.
Return the length of the longest contiguous subarray such that the absolute difference between any two elements in the subarray is less than or equal to limit.
You must solve this using an efficient sliding window approach (O(n) time).

examples.explanation: The window [2, 4] is valid (max=4, min=2, diff=2 <= 4). Extending to [2, 4, 7] breaks the limit (7-2=5). The longest valid window has length 2.
The longest valid window is [2, 4, 7, 2] with max=7, min=2, diff=5. Length = 4.
limit=0 means all values in the window must be equal. The longest run of identical values is [2,2,2] → length 3.

edge_cases: limit = 0 (forces all elements in the window to be identical)
Large ranges of values (to test monotonic queue correctness)
All elements identical (entire array is valid)
notes: A naive O(n^2) solution checking all windows will time out.
Maintaining the window's min and max efficiently is the key challenge.
Monotonic queues allow O(1) amortized updates for min/max tracking.
hints: Hint 1: You need to track the minimum and maximum inside the current window in O(1) amortized time.
Hint 2: Consider using two monotonic deques: one for the minimums, one for the maximums.
Hint 3: Expand the right pointer; shrink the left pointer only when the window becomes invalid.
Hint 4: The answer is the maximum window size observed while maintaining validity.
